TestCase Description: [Functional-Authentication] Verify successful login with valid email and password
PreCondition: User has a registered VWO account with known email/password
TestSteps: 1. Navigate to app.vwo.com login page; 2. Enter valid email; 3. Enter valid password; 4. Click Login button
Expected Result: User is authenticated and redirected to the VWO dashboard
Misc (Comments): PRD: Functional Requirements > Authentication System > Login Process - Primary Authentication

TestCase Description: [Functional-Authentication] Verify login is rejected with an incorrect password
PreCondition: User has a registered VWO account
TestSteps: 1. Navigate to login page; 2. Enter valid email; 3. Enter incorrect password; 4. Click Login
Expected Result: Login is rejected; a clear, actionable error message is displayed; user remains on login page
Misc (Comments): PRD: Login Process - secure validation; Error Handling

TestCase Description: [Functional-Authentication] Verify login is rejected for an unregistered email address
TestSteps: 1. Navigate to login page; 2. Enter an email not associated with any account; 3. Enter any password; 4. Click Login
Expected Result: Login is rejected; clear, actionable error message shown
Misc (Comments): PRD: Login Process - secure validation; Error Handling

TestCase Description: [Functional-Validation] Verify login is blocked when the email field is left empty
TestSteps: 1. Navigate to login page; 2. Leave email field empty; 3. Enter a password; 4. Click Login
Expected Result: Real-time/field validation prevents submission or shows an inline error on the email field
Misc (Comments): PRD: User Input Validation - Real-time Validation on blur

TestCase Description: [Functional-Validation] Verify login is blocked when the password field is left empty
TestSteps: 1. Navigate to login page; 2. Enter a valid email; 3. Leave password field empty; 4. Click Login
Expected Result: Real-time/field validation prevents submission or shows an inline error on the password field
Misc (Comments): PRD: User Input Validation - Real-time Validation on blur

TestCase Description: [Functional-Authentication] Verify a secure session is established after successful login
TestSteps: 1. Log in with valid email/password; 2. Inspect session token/cookie issued by the application
Expected Result: A secure session token is created and associated with the authenticated user
Misc (Comments): PRD: Session Management - secure session handling
Is Automated: No (Requires backend/session-store inspection, not pure UI)

TestCase Description: [Functional-Authentication] Verify session expires after the configured timeout period
PreCondition: User is logged in with an active session
TestSteps: 1. Log in successfully; 2. Leave the session idle beyond the configured timeout; 3. Attempt to access the dashboard
Expected Result: User session has expired and user is redirected to login / re-authentication is required
Misc (Comments): PRD: Session Management - configurable timeout periods. Exact timeout duration not specified in PRD - Inference (low confidence) on wait duration used in test.

TestCase Description: [Functional-Authentication] Verify user can opt in to enable optional Multi-Factor Authentication (2FA)
PreCondition: User is logged into account settings
TestSteps: 1. Navigate to security/account settings; 2. Enable 2FA option
Expected Result: 2FA is enabled successfully for the account
Misc (Comments): PRD: Multi-Factor Authentication - Optional 2FA support. Exact settings UI location not specified in PRD - Inference (low confidence).

TestCase Description: [Functional-Authentication] Verify user is prompted for a 2FA code after successful primary authentication when 2FA is enabled
PreCondition: 2FA is enabled on the test account
TestSteps: 1. Enter valid email and password; 2. Click Login
Expected Result: User is prompted to enter a second-factor authentication code before being granted access
Misc (Comments): PRD: Multi-Factor Authentication

TestCase Description: [Functional-Authentication] Verify login is rejected when an incorrect 2FA code is entered
PreCondition: 2FA is enabled; user has passed primary authentication
TestSteps: 1. Complete primary login; 2. Enter an incorrect 2FA code; 3. Submit
Expected Result: Access is denied; clear error message displayed; user is not granted a session
Misc (Comments): PRD: Multi-Factor Authentication; Error Handling

TestCase Description: [Functional-Authentication] Verify Enterprise SSO login option is available for organizational accounts
PreCondition: Test account belongs to an organization with SSO configured
TestSteps: 1. Navigate to login page; 2. Verify presence of SSO login option for the organizational account
Expected Result: SSO login option (e.g., 'Login with SSO') is visible and available
Misc (Comments): PRD: Single Sign-On (SSO) - Enterprise SSO integration capabilities

TestCase Description: [Functional-Authentication] Verify SSO login authenticates the user via the organization's Identity Provider
PreCondition: SSO is configured for the test organization (SAML/OAuth)
TestSteps: 1. Click SSO login option; 2. Authenticate via the Identity Provider; 3. Return to VWO
Expected Result: User is authenticated and redirected to the VWO dashboard via SSO
Misc (Comments): PRD: SSO; Third-Party Services - Enterprise SSO (SAML, OAuth)
Is Automated: No (Depends on external IdP test environment)

Test Data: Any input field on login form
TestCase Description: [Functional-Validation] Verify field-level validation triggers on blur
TestSteps: 1. Focus on email field then move focus away (blur) without valid input; 2. Observe field state
Expected Result: Field validation message/feedback is shown immediately after blur, without requiring form submission
Misc (Comments): PRD: Real-time Validation - Field validation on blur

TestCase Description: [Functional-Validation] Verify email format is validated and rejects malformed addresses
TestSteps: 1. Enter a malformed email address in the email field; 2. Move focus away or submit
Expected Result: An email-format validation error is displayed
Misc (Comments): PRD: Email Format Verification - automatic email format validation

TestCase Description: [Functional-Validation] Verify a specialized mobile keyboard (email layout) appears for the email field on mobile devices
PreCondition: Test executed on a mobile device or mobile emulator
TestSteps: 1. Open login page on a mobile device; 2. Tap the email field
Expected Result: The device presents an email-optimized keyboard (e.g., with '@' key)
Misc (Comments): PRD: Email Format Verification - specialized mobile keyboards
Is Automated: No (Mobile OS keyboard behavior not verifiable via standard Selenium WebDriver)

Test Data: Passwords of varying strength while typing
TestCase Description: [Functional-Validation] Verify password strength indicator updates dynamically as the user types
TestSteps: 1. Focus on the password field; 2. Type a weak password, then a stronger password incrementally; 3. Observe strength indicator
Expected Result: Visual password strength indicator updates in real time to reflect current input strength
Misc (Comments): PRD: Password Strength Indicators - Visual feedback for password requirements and strength. Exact strength thresholds not specified in PRD - Inference (low confidence).

TestCase Description: [Functional-Validation] Verify a clear, actionable error message is shown on failed authentication
TestSteps: 1. Attempt login with invalid credentials; 2. Observe the error message displayed
Expected Result: A clear, actionable error message is displayed, guiding the user on next steps
Misc (Comments): PRD: Error Handling - Clear, actionable error messages for failed authentication attempts

TestCase Description: [Functional-Password Management] Verify clicking 'Forgot Password' initiates the password reset flow
TestSteps: 1. On login page, click 'Forgot Password' link; 2. Enter registered email; 3. Submit
Expected Result: Password reset flow is initiated; confirmation message is shown to the user
Misc (Comments): PRD: Forgot Password Flow - Streamlined password reset process

TestCase Description: [Functional-Password Management] Verify a secure reset token is generated and delivered via email during password reset
PreCondition: User has initiated the forgot-password flow
TestSteps: 1. Submit forgot-password request for a registered email; 2. Check the email inbox for the reset link/token
Expected Result: A secure, unique reset token/link is generated and received in the user's email
Misc (Comments): PRD: Forgot Password Flow - secure token generation
Is Automated: No (Requires email inbox/API verification, not pure browser UI)

TestCase Description: [Functional-Password Management] Verify password recovery completes successfully via the emailed reset link
PreCondition: A valid reset token/link has been generated for the account
TestSteps: 1. Open the password reset link from email; 2. Enter and confirm a new password; 3. Submit
Expected Result: Password is updated successfully; user can log in with the new password
Misc (Comments): PRD: Password Recovery - Multiple recovery options including email-based reset

TestCase Description: [Functional-Password Management] Verify a new password meeting enforced complexity requirements is accepted
PreCondition: User is on the set-new-password step of password reset (or registration)
TestSteps: 1. Enter a new password that satisfies the enforced complexity rules; 2. Confirm password; 3. Submit
Expected Result: New password is accepted and saved
Misc (Comments): PRD: Password Requirements - Enforced security standards for password complexity. Exact complexity rules (length/char classes) not specified in PRD - Insufficient information to determine.

Test Data: New password NOT meeting complexity rules
TestCase Description: [Functional-Password Management] Verify a new password that fails complexity requirements is rejected
PreCondition: User is on the set-new-password step
TestSteps: 1. Enter a new password that does not satisfy complexity requirements; 2. Submit
Expected Result: Submission is rejected with a validation message indicating the password does not meet requirements
Misc (Comments): PRD: Password Requirements. Exact complexity rules not specified in PRD - Insufficient information to determine.

TestCase Description: [Functional-UX] Verify the login page renders correctly and responsively on a mobile viewport
TestSteps: 1. Open login page on a mobile viewport/device; 2. Observe layout of form elements
Expected Result: Login form and all elements render correctly, legibly, and without overlap on the mobile viewport
Misc (Comments): PRD: Responsive Design - Mobile-optimized interface with touch-friendly controls

TestCase Description: [Functional-UX] Verify all login form controls are usable via touch input
PreCondition: Test executed on a touch-enabled device or emulator
TestSteps: 1. Tap email field, password field, and Login button using touch input
Expected Result: All controls respond correctly to touch interaction
Misc (Comments): PRD: Responsive Design - touch-friendly controls
Is Automated: No (Touch-specific interaction not fully simulable via standard Selenium WebDriver)

TestCase Description: [Functional-UX] Verify the first input field (email) is auto-focused when the login page loads
TestSteps: 1. Navigate to the login page; 2. Observe which field has focus without any user interaction
Expected Result: The email input field is automatically focused on page load
Misc (Comments): PRD: Auto-focus - Automatic focus on the first input field

TestCase Description: [Functional-UX] Verify clicking a form label focuses its associated input field
TestSteps: 1. Click on the 'Email' label text (not the input itself); 2. Observe focus state
Expected Result: Clicking the label moves focus to its associated input field

TestCase Description: [Functional-UX] Verify a loading state is displayed while the authentication request is processing

TestCase Description: [Functional-Accessibility] Verify screen readers correctly announce login form fields via ARIA labels

TestCase Description: [Functional-Accessibility] Verify High Contrast Mode can be enabled and is correctly applied to the login page

TestCase Description: [Functional-Accessibility] Verify all interactive elements on the login page are reachable and operable using only the keyboard

TestCase Description: [Functional-Branding] Verify the login page's visual design aligns with VWO's overall brand design system

TestCase Description: [Non-Functional-Security] Verify all authentication data is transmitted using end-to-end encryption

Expected Result: All authentication data in transit is encrypted end-to-end; no credentials appear in plaintext
Misc (Comments): PRD: Data Protection - Encryption: End-to-end encryption for all authentication data transmission

TestCase Description: [Non-Functional-Security] Verify passwords are stored using industry-standard hashing, not in plaintext
PreCondition: Access to the credential data store (test/staging) is available for verification
TestSteps: 1. Create/reset a user password; 2. Inspect the stored password value in the data store
Expected Result: Stored password is hashed using an industry-standard algorithm; plaintext password is not stored

TestCase Description: [Non-Functional-Security] Verify session tokens are securely generated and cannot be predicted or reused after logout

TestSteps: 1. Log in and capture the session token; 2. Log out; 3. Attempt to reuse the captured token to access the dashboard

Test Data: Login page URL over plain HTTP
TestCase Description: [Non-Functional-Security] Verify HTTPS/TLS is enforced for all login page communications

Test Data: Repeated invalid login attempts from same source

Expected Result: Further login attempts are throttled or temporarily blocked, protecting against brute-force attacks
Misc (Comments): PRD: Compliance Standards - Rate Limiting: Protection against brute force attacks through request throttling. Exact attempt threshold not specified in PRD - Inference (low confidence).

TestCase Description: [Non-Functional-Security] Verify the system prevents successful brute-force compromise of an account

Expected Result: Zero successful unauthorized access occurs; account access remains protected

Is Automated: No (Security/penetration-testing exercise, not standard functional automation)
